# FIRST Robotics Coach / Lead Mentor Survey — TimeHuddle Market Research

> Copy these questions into a Google Form. Each `---` is a new section break.

**Form title**: How Do You Manage Your FIRST Robotics Team?
**Form description**: We're building a team management tool and are exploring
how it could serve FIRST Robotics teams. We want to understand how coaches and
lead mentors coordinate students, mentors, and sub-teams — especially during
build season. This takes about 7–10 minutes. No right or wrong answers.

---

## Section 1: About Your Team

**Q1 — What is your primary role on the team?**
*Multiple choice*
- Head coach / lead mentor
- Sub-team mentor (software, mechanical, electrical, etc.)
- Teacher advisor
- Parent volunteer coordinator
- Team captain (student)
- Other: ___

**Q2 — What FIRST program does your team compete in?**
*Multiple choice — pick all that apply*
- FRC (FIRST Robotics Competition)
- FTC (FIRST Tech Challenge)
- FLL Challenge (FIRST LEGO League)
- FLL Explore
- Other: ___

**Q3 — How many active students are on your team?**
*Multiple choice*
- 1–10
- 11–20
- 21–40
- More than 40

**Q4 — How many mentors (adult volunteers) are regularly involved?**
*Multiple choice*
- 1–3
- 4–8
- 9–15
- More than 15

**Q5 — Does your team have defined sub-teams?**
*Multiple choice*
- Yes — students specialize (software, mechanical, electrical, drive, business,
  scouting, etc.)
- Somewhat — loose groupings but people work across areas
- No — everyone works on everything

---

## Section 2: Build Season Coordination

**Q6 — How long is your typical build season (or most intense work period)?**
*Multiple choice*
- Less than 4 weeks
- 4–6 weeks (typical FRC build season)
- 6–10 weeks
- Year-round at varying intensity

**Q7 — During build season, how do you track what each student or sub-team is
working on day-to-day?**
*Long text — open ended (e.g. whiteboard, Trello, nothing formal)*

**Q8 — How do you run your daily or weekly team meetings during build season?**
*Long text — open ended (structure, format, who leads, how long)*

**Q9 — What's the hardest coordination problem you face during build season?**
*Long text — open ended*

**Q10 — How do you handle task dependencies across sub-teams? (e.g. software
needs the mechanical frame before they can test autonomy)**
*Long text — open ended*

**Q11 — Do students know at the start of each build meeting what they should be
working on?**
*Multiple choice*
- Yes — we assign tasks in advance and they come prepared
- Mostly — there's a plan but it often shifts
- Somewhat — they know their sub-team's area but not specific tasks
- Not really — figuring out what to do eats into build time

---

## Section 3: Attendance and Hours

**Q12 — Do you track student attendance and hours at build sessions?**
*Multiple choice*
- Yes — formally, with a sign-in system
- Yes — informally (paper sheet, spreadsheet)
- Sometimes — when we remember
- No

**Q13 — Why do you track hours? (if applicable)**
*Multiple choice — pick all that apply*
- Required by the school or program
- For award submissions (Chairman's, Engineering Inspiration, Impact Award)
- For sponsor reporting
- For student recognition (scholarships, awards)
- For our own records
- We don't track hours
- Other: ___

**Q14 — Do you track mentor/volunteer hours separately from student hours?**
*Multiple choice*
- Yes — separately
- We track them together
- We only track student hours
- We only track mentor hours
- We don't track hours

**Q15 — How painful is your current hour-tracking process?**
*Scale: 1 (completely painless) → 5 (genuinely frustrating and error-prone)*

**Q16 — Have you ever needed hours data for a grant application, sponsor report,
or award submission and struggled to pull it together accurately?**
*Multiple choice*
- Yes — frequently
- Yes — once or twice
- No, but I can see how it would happen
- No — our records are solid

---

## Section 4: Sub-Team and Student Management

**Q17 — How do mentors know which students to work with on a given build day?**
*Long text — open ended*

**Q18 — Do students who miss a build session know what they missed and what to
catch up on?**
*Multiple choice*
- Yes — we have a consistent communication channel for this
- Sometimes — it depends on their sub-team lead
- Rarely — catching up is mostly informal
- No — missing a session means you're just behind

**Q19 — How do you manage students who work across multiple sub-teams?**
*Long text — open ended*

**Q20 — What information do you wish you had about each student's contributions
at the end of the season?**
*Long text — open ended (e.g. hours by sub-team, tasks completed, skills
demonstrated)*

---

## Section 5: Competition Logistics

**Q21 — How do you manage competition assignments? (pit crew, drive team,
scouting rotations)**
*Long text — open ended*

**Q22 — Do you use any digital tools to coordinate during a competition event
(away from your build space)?**
*Multiple choice — pick all that apply*
- GroupMe / WhatsApp / Slack
- Scouting app (e.g. The Blue Alliance, custom spreadsheet)
- Paper / whiteboard
- Shared Google Doc or Sheet
- No specific tools
- Other: ___

---

## Section 6: Documentation and Awards

**Q23 — Do you submit any FIRST awards that require documenting team
activities, community outreach, or engineering process?**
*Multiple choice — pick all that apply*
- Chairman's Award / Impact Award
- Engineering Inspiration Award
- Rookie All-Star
- Dean's List nomination
- Other award that requires a submission
- We don't submit awards requiring documentation
- We plan to but find the documentation burden daunting

**Q24 — How do you currently document your team's outreach, build progress, and
student development for these awards?**
*Long text — open ended*

**Q25 — If your team's activity data — hours, tasks completed, outreach events,
student milestones — was automatically captured throughout the season, how much
easier would award submissions be?**
*Scale: 1 (wouldn't help — we have other barriers) → 5 (would be
transformative for our award process)*

---

## Section 7: Tools and Technology

**Q26 — What tools does your team currently use to manage build season work?**
*Multiple choice — pick all that apply*
- GitHub (for code)
- Trello / Asana / Monday
- Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Drive)
- Slack / Discord
- Paper / whiteboard
- A FIRST-specific tool (e.g. FRC-specific apps)
- Nothing formal
- Other: ___

**Q27 — What's the biggest gap in your current toolset — the thing you most
wish you had software help with?**
*Long text — open ended*

**Q28 — FIRST teams are unique: students rotate in and out across seasons,
mentors are volunteers with day jobs, and intensity spikes during build season.
Do you think tools designed for professional software teams could work for a
FIRST team with some adaptation, or does FIRST need something purpose-built?**
*Multiple choice*
- Professional tools work fine with some configuration
- They mostly work but miss some key FIRST-specific things
- FIRST teams need something purpose-built
- Not sure

---

## Section 8: AI and Future Possibilities

**Q29 — Would automated student progress summaries — "Alex attended 18 of 22
build sessions, contributed primarily to software, and completed 3 autonomous
routines" — be useful for end-of-season recognition or award writing?**
*Scale: 1 (not useful) → 5 (extremely useful)*

**Q30 — What would you most want an AI assistant to help with on your FIRST
team?**
*Multiple choice — pick up to 3*
- Drafting award submission narratives from team activity data
- Tracking which students are falling behind or disengaging
- Suggesting task assignments based on student skills and availability
- Generating sponsor reports from hours and activity data
- Creating a build season timeline / project plan from game manual requirements
- Summarizing what each sub-team accomplished this week
- Other: ___

---

## Section 9: Anything Else

**Q31 — What's the single thing that would most improve how your team operates
during build season?**
*Long text — open ended*

**Q32 — Would you be open to a short follow-up conversation (15–20 minutes) to
go deeper on any of these topics?**
*Multiple choice*
- Yes — here's my email: ___
- Maybe — reach out and I'll decide
- No thanks

**Q33 — Any other thoughts you'd like to share?**
*Long text — optional*
